content:
{
  "title": "Diagram-Conditioned Prompting for Enhanced Mathematical Reasoning in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often falter on complex mathematical problems that require structured, multi-step reasoning, especially in geometry and algebra where visual components are critical. Traditional text-only prompts underutilize LLMs' potential to leverage spatial and relational information.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLMs' mathematical reasoning capabilities is crucial for applications in education, automated theorem proving, and scientific discovery. With recent advances in multimodal LLMs capable of processing both text and images, now is an opportune time to develop prompting strategies that better harness this capacity.",
  "method": "We propose 'Diagram-Conditioned Prompting' (DCP): for each problem, generate or extract a schematic diagram or table representing key entities and their relationships, provided alongside the usual problem text. Prompts explicitly instruct the model to refer to both modalities to construct stepwise solutions, e.g., 'Refer to the diagram and explain how each element informs your answer.' We further augment prompts with targeted sub-questions (generated automatically via retrieval over similar problems) to scaffold reasoning through intermediate steps.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate DCP on the MathQA and SAT-Math datasets, focusing on questions with inherent spatial or tabular structure. Baselines include standard text-only few-shot prompts and CoT (Chain-of-Thought) prompting. Metrics are exact answer accuracy and stepwise reasoning correctness, assessed via automatic and human evaluation."
}
